If a finding emerges and documents an undisclosed operation, this needs to become a new unknown, which mutates our conception of IDENTITY. It's not subjected to promotion because it's a live fact.
Promotion should gate only representation fidelity and applied-control fidelity, not live observations or undisclosed operations.
Undisclosed operations become new unknowns that mutate live identity immediately and are not promotion-gated.
